label: Localized knee brace contact rash without infection or anaphylaxis
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of localized knee brace contact skin reaction.
No anaphylaxis, systemic allergic reaction, mucosal involvement, skin infection, neurovascular compromise, severe medication reaction, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Anaphylaxis, airway symptoms, hypotension, systemic allergic reaction, mucosal lesions, severe cutaneous adverse reaction, skin infection, rapidly progressive rash, neurovascular compromise, immunocompromised host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed dermatology/allergy/orthopedic plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, dermatology, allergy, orthopedics, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports contact dermatitis framing from irritant or allergic exposures, trigger avoidance, gentle skin care, clinician-directed topical or allergy treatment, and escalation for severe allergic, infectious, mucosal, or circulation red flags. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed localized knee brace contact skin reaction with anaphylaxis, infection, mucosal, neurovascular, pregnancy/pediatric, severe-rash, and specialist-directed pathways absent.

terms: clinician diagnosis localized knee brace contact rash
discharge diagnosis localized knee brace contact rash
localized knee brace contact rash diagnosed
